published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML and SemenovaChernozhukov2021DMLCATE: AIPW/DML orthogonal score identity and product-remainder control

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dml_orth_uniform/v2: spectral studentization-report frontier, not Holder/entropy first-order HTE DML breakdown

KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2024MinimaxCATE and ArmstrongKolesar2021FiniteSampleATE give smoothness/minimax limitations, but not this first-order DML HTE report-class exponent frontier

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The expansion hides the nuisance empirical-process remainder under the undefined phrase 'uniformly up to the empirical-process terms controlled by Assumption crossfit'; state the missing term explicitly or add a precise assumption that its L_n supremum is o_p(n^{-1/2}).

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Patch Theorem 1 by displaying the full decomposition including (P_n-P)[ell(X){Gamma(W;hat eta)-Gamma(W;eta_0)}] and naming the exact condition under which it is o_p(n^{-1/2}) uniformly over L_n.
Add Newey-Robins cross-fitting/fast-remainder work as an additional non-flagship comparator for the plug-in remainder theorem, while keeping the flagship gap on the iff lower-bound frontier.
Strengthen the witness example by giving the resolution J_n and coefficient scaling that produce E_n asymptotic n^zeta and c_R(P)>c0 on an explicit open coefficient set.

recommended_next_step: Revise locally by making Theorem 1's empirical-process remainder mathematically explicit; the flagship novelty kernel otherwise survives both repo and published checks.
